scaffold_status: Block A analytical-floor scaffold per master 2026-05-21 directive (post OpenEMS UNAVAILABLE).
post_gui_steps: 1. Re-run sims/extract_trace_geometry.py against the post-GUI novapcb-layout-v2.kicad_pcb to refresh trace_geometry.json.
2. Re-run this script — verdicts will populate from TBD_POST_GUI.
3. Cross-check 6c IMU SPI overshoot — if still >200 mV, add 22Ω series-term R footprint (would be a Step 6 design change).
4. Ping master with final Block A results + 6b USB verdict.

6b.name: 6b_usb_diff_pair
6b.method: Hammerstad-Jensen + IPC-2141 edge-coupled + analytical via-transition reflection model
6b.criterion: USB 2.0 ±15% window [76.5, 103.5] Ω at the segment level; |S11| <= -15 dB cumulative at 480 MHz including via discontinuities
6b.via_transition_model: discrete impedance step. Reflection coefficient Γ = (Z2-Z1)/(Z2+Z1) at each F.Cu↔B.Cu via. Cumulative |S11| ≈ 20*log10(|Γ| * fudge_factor) where fudge_factor accounts for back-to-back via reflections partially canceling. For JLC06161H-7628 symmetric stackup, F.Cu and B.Cu are both 94.4Ω so via transitions are impedance-matched per master 2026-05-21 — Γ=0 from impedance mismatch; only the via's own stub-and-pad capacitance contributes.

via_transition.discontinuity_freq_band: well above USB 2.0 480 MHz (Lpar/Cpar resonance ~ GHz range for 0.4nH/100fF)

6b.bench_dependency: Phase 9 fab impedance coupon + bench TDR is the final verdict per Phase 0.6 floor.

6c.name: 6c_imu_spi
6c.criterion: SIMULATION_PLAN §6c: rise/fall <5 ns; setup/hold margin >2 ns; ringing <200 mV
6c.notes: Routed lengths around 36-45 mm. At 24 MHz SPI clock + STM32H743 GPIO ~2ns edge, the trace is lumped (TOF ~0.3 ns << edge 2 ns). Earlier Block A pre-reroute sim showed modeled overshoot 354-489 mV due to lumped LRC tank with 30Ω driver. Final 6c run after GUI pass will use updated geometry; if overshoot persists, consider 22Ω series term R at MCU pins (D1/§2 review).

6f.name: 6f_sdmmc
6f.criterion: SIMULATION_PLAN §6f: SDR25 12.5 MHz clean clock + setup/hold met
6f.notes: 12.5 MHz SDR25 with ~3ns edge — lumped regime. Pre-reroute Block A: 6/6 PASS. Post-GUI re-run expected same result; trace geometry barely shifts.

6g.name: 6g_dshot
6g.criterion: SIMULATION_PLAN §6g: DShot600 settling <0.83 us; no >300mV ringing
6g.notes: DShot600 (600 kHz fundamental, 1.67 us bit period) is electrically tiny at our trace lengths. Pre-reroute Block A: 8/8 PASS. Post-GUI re-run expected same.
